meta.name: Foundation Blog Posts
meta.description: Blog and announcement content from the Foundation NFT marketplace (foundation.app). Foundation permanently shut down on April 27, 2026 after failing to complete a third-party sale. Smart contracts remain live on Ethereum mainnet and the IPFS gateway is maintained through April 27, 2027.

posts.title: Foundation Platform Shutdown Announcement
posts.description: Foundation Labs announced the permanent closure of the Foundation NFT marketplace. The company attempted to sell the platform to a third party but the transaction did not complete. User NFTs remain in wallets as the platform operated as a non-custodial on-chain system. Foundation will maintain their IPFS gateway through April 27, 2027 to allow a one-year transition period for media preservation.

posts.title: Foundation Protocol GitHub Repository
posts.description: Open-source Ethereum smart contract protocol underpinning the Foundation NFT marketplace. Includes Solidity contracts for NFT minting, timed auctions, buy-now listings, percent splits, and FETH escrow. Contracts remain deployed and operational on Ethereum mainnet.

posts.title: Foundation Subgraph on The Graph Protocol
posts.description: Foundation's GraphQL subgraph deployed on The Graph hosted service indexes all on-chain NFT marketplace activity including auctions, bids, offers, transfers, and percent splits. Developers can query this endpoint to access real-time and historical Foundation marketplace data programmatically.

posts.title: Foundation Protocol Examples
posts.description: Official code examples for interacting with the Foundation NFT protocol. Demonstrates how to query the subgraph GraphQL API and interact with Foundation smart contracts programmatically.

posts.title: Foundation Bug Bounty on Immunefi
posts.description: Foundation maintained a bug bounty program through Immunefi for responsible disclosure of security vulnerabilities in Foundation smart contracts. Smart contracts remain live on Ethereum mainnet.
